fullTitle: Security for Industrial Automation and Control Systems (IACS)
publisher: International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) / International Society of Automation (ISA)
verificationNote: Content paraphrased from IEC 62443 series. Obtain normative text from iec.ch or isa.org. Clause references provided for source traceability.
jurisdiction: International — referenced by NACSA Act 854 codes of practice for OT-heavy NCII sectors (energy, water, transport, manufacturing)
scope: Defines requirements for the security of Industrial Automation and Control Systems (IACS) across the entire system lifecycle — from policies and procedures through system design to component security. Applicable to any organisation that owns, operates, integrates or supplies components to an IACS.
malaysiaNexus: NCII-designated entities in OT-heavy sectors (energy, water, oil-gas, transport, manufacturing) are expected to use IEC 62443 as the baseline cybersecurity framework for their operational technology environments. NACSA Act 854 s18 and s21 obligations are substantially met through IEC 62443 SL 2 compliance for most NCII sectors.

series.title: General
series.description: Common concepts, terminology, metrics and models applicable across the entire series.

parts.title: Terminology, concepts and models
parts.keyContent: Defines IACS, zones, conduits, security levels (SL), foundational requirements (FR). Core vocabulary for the entire standard.

parts.title: Master glossary of terms and abbreviations
System security conformance metrics
parts.keyContent: Quantitative metrics for measuring security conformance.

parts.title: IACS security lifecycle and use cases
parts.keyContent: Lifecycle model covering design, integration, operation, maintenance, decommission.

series.title: Policies and Procedures
series.description: Requirements for asset owners — how to manage IACS security through policies, procedures, risk assessment and patch management.

parts.title: Requirements for an IACS security management system
parts.keyContent: IACS-equivalent of ISO 27001. Security management programme, risk assessment, security plan, awareness training, incident response.

parts.title: IACS protection levels
parts.keyContent: Operating security levels, protection levels, monitoring.

parts.title: Patch management in the IACS environment
parts.keyContent: Patch management lifecycle specific to OT — covering vendor patch availability, testing, deployment in constrained OT environments.

parts.title: Requirements for IACS solution suppliers
parts.keyContent: Security requirements for system integrators and service providers who install or maintain IACS.

series.title: System
series.description: System-level security requirements — zone and conduit design, security risk assessment, system security requirements.

parts.title: Security technologies for IACS
parts.keyContent: Survey of applicable security technologies (authentication, encryption, firewalls, IDS) in the OT context.

parts.title: Security risk assessment for system design
parts.keyContent: Methodology for zone and conduit definition, security level targeting (SL-T), threat and risk assessment for system design.

parts.title: System security requirements and security levels
parts.keyContent: The primary compliance checklist. Defines 51 System Requirements (SRs) under 7 Foundational Requirements (FRs), each specified at SL 1 through SL 4.

series.title: Component
series.description: Component-level security — requirements for product developers manufacturing controllers, sensors, software components used in IACS.

parts.title: Product security development lifecycle requirements
parts.keyContent: Secure development lifecycle for IACS product manufacturers: security management, threat modelling, secure design, verification, defect management.

parts.title: Technical security requirements for IACS components
parts.keyContent: Component-level SRs for embedded devices, host devices, network devices and software applications used within IACS.

keyConceptSummary.iacs: Industrial Automation and Control System — the collective term for all systems used to control and monitor industrial processes. Includes SCADA, DCS, PLC-based systems, safety systems and supporting infrastructure.
keyConceptSummary.zone: A logical grouping of assets with the same security level target and security policies. Assets within a zone are protected by the same set of security controls.
keyConceptSummary.conduit: A communication path or grouping of communication channels that shares the same security characteristics. Conduits connect zones and are the primary mechanism for controlling inter-zone data flow.
keyConceptSummary.sl: Security Level. A discrete measure of protection required (SL-T: Target) or achieved (SL-A: Achieved) against specific threat categories. Ranges from SL 1 (low) to SL 4 (extreme).
keyConceptSummary.fr: Foundational Requirement. One of 7 high-level security objectives (IAC, UC, SI, DC, RDF, TRE, RA) that organise the 51 System Requirements.
keyConceptSummary.sr: System Requirement. A specific, testable security requirement derived from an FR. Defined at each SL with increasing stringency.
